mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in 70% of periods where industrial production growth accelerates, invalidating the short-term momentum link.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5 percent annualized forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses the 3.5 percent threshold across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than half of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of market sentiment.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces near-term corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of periods following two consecutive months of industrial production declines over a 10-year sample.
